name: ASU Tutoring
description: Free academic help in writing, math, science, business and other subjects through peer tutors online and across ASU campuses.
location: In-person support centers across ASU campuses and online tutoring spaces.
hours: Hours vary by subject and campus. Check the tutoring schedule for the latest openings.
signUpSummary: Most tutoring is drop-in. Writing support and some specialty services use scheduled appointments.

flowSteps.title: Open tutoring.asu.edu
flowSteps.description: Start on the Academic Support Network site and pick the tutoring option that matches your course.

flowSteps.title: Choose your subject or tutoring type
flowSteps.description: Look for your class, writing support, or the online tutoring menu.

flowSteps.title: Pick a time or drop-in option
flowSteps.description: Some services are scheduled and some are open drop-in while the center is running.

flowSteps.title: Bring your class materials
flowSteps.description: Show up with your homework, notes, or draft so the tutor can meet you where you are.

flowSteps.title: Join the session
flowSteps.description: You will work with a peer tutor and leave with a clearer next step for the assignment.

name: Academic Advising
description: Academic advisors help with class schedules, degree requirements, major questions, and planning your next semester.
location: Appointments happen through your college advising office in MyASU, often online or on your campus.
hours: Most advising offices keep weekday appointment times. Check MyASU for your college's schedule.
signUpSummary: Use MyASU or your advising office page to schedule an appointment, then bring your questions and degree concerns.

flowSteps.title: Go to MyASU or the advising page
flowSteps.description: Open your advising link from MyASU or your college's advising website.

flowSteps.title: Select an advising appointment
flowSteps.description: Pick the reason for the visit, like schedule planning, changing majors, or registration questions.

flowSteps.title: Choose a date and format
flowSteps.description: Book the time that works for you and confirm whether it is virtual, phone, or in person.

flowSteps.title: Show up with your questions
flowSteps.description: Bring your current schedule, goals, and any confusing holds or deadlines you want explained.

name: ASU Counseling Services
description: Confidential counseling and crisis support for stress, anxiety, overwhelm, transitions, and other concerns affecting school or life.
location: Counseling support is available across ASU campuses, with phone support for urgent concerns.
hours: Appointment windows vary by campus. Check the counseling site for current clinic and crisis support details.
signUpSummary: Start through the counseling website or contact options, answer a few intake questions, and get matched to the right next step.

flowSteps.title: Open the counseling services site
flowSteps.description: Review the counseling overview and find the contact or appointment option that fits your need.

flowSteps.title: Choose urgent or routine support
flowSteps.description: If it is an emergency, use crisis support. Otherwise start the regular appointment process.

flowSteps.title: Complete intake details
flowSteps.description: Share basic information about what is going on so the team can route you appropriately.

flowSteps.title: Attend your first conversation
flowSteps.description: Your first session focuses on what you need and what kind of support would feel useful next.

name: Financial Aid
description: ASU financial aid explains FAFSA, aid eligibility, contributor requirements, and the follow-up steps that show up in MyASU.
location: Start online through ASU Tuition and MyASU. Additional help is available through ASU financial aid contacts.
hours: Most online guidance is always available. Office contact and advising hours vary by term and office.
signUpSummary: Complete the FAFSA or CSS Profile if needed, then watch MyASU for tasks, verification, and award updates.

flowSteps.title: Read the FAFSA guidance
flowSteps.description: Start on ASU's FAFSA page so you know which form applies to your situation.

flowSteps.title: Go to StudentAid.gov
flowSteps.description: Complete the FAFSA and gather the tax, income, and contributor information you need.

flowSteps.title: Submit and monitor MyASU
flowSteps.description: After filing, check your ASU tasks regularly for verification requests or missing documents.

flowSteps.title: Review your award steps
flowSteps.description: Accept aid, complete remaining tasks, and follow the timing ASU gives you for disbursement or follow-up.

name: ASU Scholarship Search
description: ASU scholarship search tools help students look for merit, need-based, major-specific, and school-level scholarship opportunities.
location: Online only through ASU scholarship and tuition pages.
hours: Available online anytime. Application deadlines depend on each scholarship.
signUpSummary: Search available scholarships, read the criteria carefully, and apply before each individual deadline.

flowSteps.title: Open the scholarship search page
flowSteps.description: Start from ASU's scholarships overview and review the options tied to your college or student profile.

flowSteps.title: Filter by fit
flowSteps.description: Look for awards that match your major, residency, year, and financial need status.

flowSteps.title: Check each deadline
flowSteps.description: Every scholarship has its own date and application requirements, so note those before you start.

flowSteps.title: Submit the application
flowSteps.description: Complete the scholarship application or portal step for the opportunities that match you best.

name: Career and Professional Development Services
description: Career support helps with resumes, internships, part-time jobs, interviews, Handshake listings, and making a career plan.
location: Career and student employment support is offered online and across ASU campuses.
hours: Appointment times and employer events vary. Check the current career services schedule online.
signUpSummary: Start with the jobs and careers pages, then use career services or Handshake for appointments, postings, and next steps.

flowSteps.title: Open the jobs and careers page
flowSteps.description: Review the options for internships, part-time work, resume help, and career planning.

flowSteps.title: Choose the support you need
flowSteps.description: Decide whether you are looking for job listings, a resume review, interview prep, or a career appointment.

flowSteps.title: Use the matching platform
flowSteps.description: That may mean Handshake, a career center appointment, or the student employment process.

flowSteps.title: Follow through on the next action
flowSteps.description: Upload your materials, book the meeting, or apply to the role directly from the listed system.

name: Student Success Center
description: Student Success Center coaching helps students build routines, stay organized, and connect with support before small problems turn into big ones.
location: Student Success Center coaching is offered across ASU campuses, online, and through pop-up coaching events.
hours: Coaching hours and event times vary by campus and semester.
signUpSummary: Find a coaching option or event on the Student Success Center site and choose the format that feels easiest to try.

flowSteps.title: Open success.asu.edu
flowSteps.description: Review coaching, events, and pop-up support options from the Student Success Center home page.

flowSteps.title: Pick a support style
flowSteps.description: Choose between a coaching appointment, event, or low-pressure pop-up conversation.

flowSteps.title: Register or note the event details
flowSteps.description: Save the location, time, or meeting link so you know exactly what happens next.

flowSteps.title: Show up and talk it through
flowSteps.description: The first conversation usually focuses on your routine, stress points, and the campus tools that could help most.
